message.content:
You are auditing the Claudinite repo at /home/user/Claudinite for a "check-fix subsumption audit" (agent-preprocessing DESIGN §11, from #405).

BACKGROUND: Claudinite's per-repo `baselining` task used to run an AGENT "align" step that, for every failing conformance check, applied that check's own `fix` remedy. We are moving baselining so a deterministic PREPROCESSING worker handles the mechanical work and the agent runs only when judgment is left. The deterministic preprocessing performs exactly these four kinds of repair:
  (1) CONVERGE — rebuild `.claudinite/shared/` to the canon vendor set (vendoring/apply-vendor-set.mjs): fixes any drift where a vendored/mounted file differs from canon.
  (2) CONVERGE-WIRING (engine/scheduler/converge-wiring.mjs): fixes the scheduler workflow (.github/workflows/claudinite-scheduler.yml + its hashed cron), the settings.json SessionStart/Stop/PreToolUse hook registrations, and removal of the retired `@.claudinite/shared/CLAUDE.md` corpus-index import.
  (3) DECLARATION-NORMALIZATION: rewrites a local pack's declaration token in .claudinite-checks.json to the canonical `local/<name>` form.
  (4) MECHANICAL MIGRATIONS (migrations/apply.mjs): file aliases/renames, materialize (vendor pack templates into the repo), and literal ref rewrites declared by active migration records.

GOAL: Enumerate EVERY conformance check in the repo and classify each by whether the drift it flags is SUBSUMED by one of those four deterministic repairs, or whether it NEEDS JUDGMENT (an agent/human).

WHERE CHECKS LIVE: check rule modules are `*.mjs` files (NOT `*.test.mjs`) that export a rule object with an `id`, a `severity` ('blocking'|'advisory'), and a `run(ctx)` that returns findings via `finding(rule, {file, what, fix})`. They live under `packs/*/*.mjs`, `packs/*/skills/*/*.mjs`, `engine/checks/**`, and `.claudinite/local/packs/*/*.mjs`. Some checks are skill-owned (inside a pack's skills/<name>/). Use grep for `severity:` and `id:` and `finding(` to find them. Read each rule's `id`, `severity`, `description`, `why`, its `run()` logic, and the `fix` strings it emits.

Also check: does `engine/checks/check_the_world.mjs` run the SAME set of checks (so a failing check would always be caught by the check_the_world escalation gate)? Note any check that runs only in a different scope (e.g. work-scope only, or CI-only) and would NOT appear in a whole-repo check_the_world sweep.

For EACH check, produce a record. Classify `category` as exactly one of:
  - "converge"            (drift = a vendored/mounted file differs from canon; converge fixes it)
  - "converge-wiring"     (scheduler workflow / hooks / retired import)
  - "declaration-normalization"
  - "migrations"          (legacy shape a migration record mechanically converts)
  - "needs-judgment"      (code quality, author-written doc/reference integrity, file placement, naming, test discipline, security, or anything requiring a real decision — NOT mechanically fixable by the four repairs)
And set `subsumed` = true for the first four categories, false for needs-judgment.
For needs-judgment checks, also set `mechanicalFix` = true ONLY if the check's `fix` is actually a rote/mechanical edit that the old baselining would have auto-applied AND that is NOT yet covered by the four deterministic repairs (i.e. a candidate to make deterministic later); otherwise false.

Return your final answer as STRICT JSON (no prose around it) of shape:
{
  "checkTheWorldRunsAll": <boolean>,
  "checkTheWorldNotes": "<short note on any check NOT in the world sweep>",
  "checks": [
    {"id": "...", "pack": "...", "severity": "blocking|advisory", "category": "...", "subsumed": <bool>, "mechanicalFix": <bool>, "detects": "<one line: what drift it flags>", "rationale": "<one line: why this category>"}
  ]
}
Be exhaustive — every check module. If you are unsure of a classification, pick the closest and say so in rationale. Do not skip skill-owned checks or engine/checks/ checks.

- xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .xltx, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- adopt-claudinite: Bootstrap Claudinite into a consuming repo — mount, hooks, checks, skills. Use when asked to bootstrap, adopt, or set up Claudinite, or to baseline a repo to pick up updates.
- authoring-agent-docs: How to write instruction files coding agents follow reliably. Use before writing or editing any Claude instruction doc — a project CLAUDE.md, a convention doc, a routine spec.
- bug-investigation: Method for investigating a bug and pinning down its root cause. Use when investigating a bug report, when a fix didn't hold or a bug recurs, or when a report doesn't reproduce against main.
- bump-version: Raise the project's version — a minor bump by default. Use when the owner says "bump version".
- engineering-practices: General software-engineering practices — naming by scope, single-source-of-truth with drift guards, GENERATED-file discipline, earning each dependency, verifying real platform behavior. Use before writing or editing code.
- file-placement: Where a file should live — the reference-distance metric, the high-reach code smell, and the mandated-location and test-location exemptions. Use before placing, moving, or renaming a file, or when reviewing where one lives.
- generate-project-instructions: Decompose a project into its pack facets (working-style class, technology, aspect, domain) and extract its working instructions into reusable canon packs plus the project's own local packs (.claudinite/local_packs/). Use when a project has no established working style yet, or it exhibits a facet no pack covers — during bootstrap of a fresh/empty repo, or on an uplevel request.
- prose-to-checks: Mine the corpus's existing prose (pack RULES.md, skill SKILL.md) for always-testable rules that were never converted to checks, and convert the strongest ones. Use when auditing the corpus for convertible rules, or when the nightly growth routine runs its prose-to-checks sweep.
- repo-text-sweeps: Mechanics for grep/sed sweeps, renames, and path relocations across a repo. Use before a bulk find-replace, a rename, or moving files — and after one, to catch silently broken references.
- unattended-agents: Architecture and practices for unattended, automation-invoked agents and recurring routines. Use when building, structuring, or running an AI agent, a scheduled routine, or a multi-stage agent pipeline.

Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".

Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. - simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.

aws-sam/cloudfront-authorization	blocking	A custom CloudFront OriginRequestPolicy may not list Authorization in its headers	packs/aws-sam/RULES.md
aws-sam/esbuild-dependency	blocking	esbuild must be a regular dependency, not a devDependency, for SAM esbuild builds	packs/aws-sam/RULES.md
aws-sam/handler-path	blocking	A SAM esbuild single-entry Handler must not carry the entry subdirectory	packs/aws-sam/RULES.md
barrier	blocking	Folders must not reference across a declared barrier (the barriers pack entry's config)	packs/barriers/README.md
catalog-completeness	blocking	packs/README.md lists every packs/<name>/	packs/README.md
cer/permission-added-store-issue	advisory	A permission added to the manifest needs a manual store-dashboard justification — open a tracking issue	packs/chrome-extension-release/RELEASE.md
cer/privacy-permission-alignment	blocking	Every manifest permission is disclosed in the privacy document	packs/chrome-extension-release/RELEASE.md
cer/readme-sections	blocking	The README carries the standard Install and Releasing sections	packs/chrome-extension-release/RELEASE.md
cer/release-config	blocking	.github/release.config exists and sets exactly the required release keys	packs/chrome-extension-release/RELEASE.md
cer/release-layout	blocking	The privacy policy source lives at the standard store_artifacts path	packs/chrome-extension-release/RELEASE.md
cer/release-workflows	blocking	The orchestrator (chrome-extension-release.yml, named "Release to Chrome Store", daily at the contract cron) and the reusable workflows + composite actions it calls must be vendored into .github/	packs/chrome-extension-release/RELEASE.md
cer/template-tokens	blocking	No __TOKEN__ placeholder may survive in the release workflows	packs/chrome-extension-release/RELEASE.md
cer/version-sync	blocking	The manifest's version is the single source of truth; package.json must equal it	packs/chrome-extension-release/RELEASE.md
claude-md-length	advisory	A CLAUDE.md over ~200 lines costs context and reduces adherence	skills/authoring-agent-docs/SKILL.md
claudinite-isolation	blocking	Outside the wiring files (CLAUDE.md, .claude/, .gitignore, .gitattributes, .github/workflows/, the settings file), nothing may reference .claudinite/ — inline what you need instead of importing the canon	vendoring/DESIGN.md
comment-classification	blocking	The reply to the owner's latest comment must declare an explicit `Comment class:` line	packs/basics/RULES.md
feature-requirements-first	blocking	A feature run must land an independent requirements-doc commit before its first code commit	packs/executable-requirements/RULES.md
file-placement	advisory	A code file should mostly reference files at folder distance 0–2; distance 3+ is reach	skills/file-placement/SKILL.md
generated-merge-driver	advisory	A GENERATED file needs a .gitattributes merge=ours entry	skills/engineering-practices/SKILL.md
gha/checkout-submodules	blocking	In a repo with submodules, every actions/checkout must pass submodules: true	skills/git-github-advanced/SKILL.md
gha/label-create-before-add	advisory	A workflow applying a label should create it idempotently first	skills/git-github-advanced/SKILL.md
gha/no-scheduled-fleet-executor	blocking	The vendored claudinite-scheduler.yml is the repo's only permitted cron once present; before cutover, a Claudinite executor (one that calls a canon reusable) must be workflow_dispatch-only	routines/fleet/scheduling.md
gha/pages-artifact-symlinks	blocking	A GitHub Pages artifact that uploads the repo root must prune the agent-tooling dirs (their skill symlinks dangle in CI)	bootstrap.md
gha/run-pipefail	blocking	A workflow piping in run: steps needs shell: bash — the default shell has no pipefail	skills/git-github-advanced/SKILL.md
gha/scheduled-failure-escalation	advisory	A scheduled workflow must escalate its own failure to a human-visible state	skills/git-github-advanced/SKILL.md
gha/secrets-in-job-if	blocking	A job-level if: cannot read secrets.* — the job fails red instead of skipping	skills/git-github-advanced/SKILL.md
gha/unique-automation-branch	advisory	An automated job needs a per-run-unique branch name, not a date-keyed one	skills/git-github-advanced/SKILL.md
google-client-id-single-origin	advisory	A change does not add a copy of a Google OAuth client-id literal that already lives in another file	skills/google-id-token-validation/SKILL.md
google-token-audience-pinned	blocking	A validator config declaring the Google accounts issuer pins a non-empty expected audience	skills/google-id-token-validation/SKILL.md
google-token-email-verified	blocking	Handler code trusts a Google ID token's email claim only when email_verified says so, compared as the authorizer delivers it	skills/google-id-token-validation/SKILL.md
growth-config	blocking	The grow_with_claudinite entry's config takes only retention_days (positive integer) and promote (boolean)	packs/grow_with_claudinite/README.md
in-session-github-access	blocking	In-session routine code reaches GitHub through the MCP tools, not a REST client + GITHUB_TOKEN	skills/unattended-agents/SKILL.md
interview-answer-stale	advisory	A stored adoption-interview answer names a question its pack still declares	packs/README.md
markdown-link-labels	blocking	A Markdown link whose visible label is a path must agree with its target	skills/repo-text-sweeps/SKILL.md
node/earn-each-dependency	advisory	A newly added package.json dependency should be earned — prefer a built-in or a few lines for a narrow job	skills/engineering-practices/SKILL.md
pack-independence	blocking	A pack's code imports only its own files and the engine surface — another pack's abilities arrive through declaration (requires) and configuration, never by importing its code	extending.md
pack-no-enforcement-narration	blocking	A pack's injected prose neither tells the reader to run the checks runner nor names the rules the pack's own checks enforce	engine/checks/DESIGN.md
product-wiki-freshness	advisory	A wiki page whose newest Growth log entry is older than 45 days needs a growth pass	packs/product-wiki/README.md
product-wiki-growth-log	blocking	Every Growth log entry is a dated bullet (real YYYY-MM-DD), and a seeded page has at least one	packs/product-wiki/README.md
product-wiki-isolation	blocking	Nothing outside product-wiki/ may reference the self-growing wiki folders — product-wiki/product-requirements is the only crossing point	packs/product-wiki/README.md
product-wiki-layout	blocking	A product-wiki repo carries the product-wiki/ skeleton: the index README and the reviewed product-requirements sink	packs/product-wiki/README.md
product-wiki-page-sections	blocking	Every wiki page carries Sources, Growth log, and Open questions sections	packs/product-wiki/README.md
product-wiki-sources	blocking	Every Sources bullet carries its real URL	packs/product-wiki/README.md
python-optional-import-install-hint	advisory	A guarded optional import that re-raises does so with a `pip install` hint naming the extra, not a bare ImportError	skills/python-optional-deps/SKILL.md
python-optional-import-top-level	blocking	A declared-optional dependency is imported lazily (inside the code that needs it), never at module top level	skills/python-optional-deps/SKILL.md
reference-integrity	blocking	Relative Markdown links must resolve, and no tracked file may reference a deleted path	skills/repo-text-sweeps/SKILL.md
routine-structure	blocking	A routine/task folder has a routine.md or task.md entry point wired to the scripts it invokes	skills/unattended-agents/SKILL.md
scheduler-workflow-shape	blocking	The vendored claudinite-scheduler.yml is a thin shim: hourly :10–:50 cron, concurrency, workflow_dispatch, calls the engine	packs/basics/scheduled-tasks.md
shared-constants	blocking	A value copied across files that can't share an import must appear the declared number of times in each (and, in regex mode, be identical everywhere)	skills/engineering-practices/SKILL.md
skill-no-enforcement-narration	blocking	A corpus SKILL.md neither tells the reader to run the checks runner nor names the rules its own checks enforce	.claudinite/local/packs/canon-curation/skills/writing-claudinite-skills/SKILL.md
squash-merge-history	blocking	A change introduces no merge commits (it lands squashed, not merged)	skills/merge-to-main/SKILL.md
task-declaration-shape	blocking	A tasks/<name>/task.mjs default-exports the full task contract (id, frequency, precondition_signals, agent_model, expected_outcome, agent_instructions, precondition) with legal enum values; an agentic task bounds its run with agent_execution_timeout, and any agent_preprocessing carries a timeout and stays task-local	packs/basics/scheduled-tasks.md
task-lifecycle	blocking	Work on a branch must reference a GitHub issue in at least one commit message	packs/basics/RULES.md
warning-suppression	blocking	A warning suppression must carry its reason at the site — inline or in the comment above, not a bare mute	packs/basics/RULES.md
web-speech-capture-released-on-pagehide	blocking	A repo that opens the microphone releases it on pagehide (bfcache-safe teardown), not just on implicit page unload	skills/web-speech-io/SKILL.md
web-speech-no-window-api-in-service-worker	blocking	An MV3 background service worker does not reference a Window-scoped Web Speech API (recognition or speechSynthesis)	skills/web-speech-io/SKILL.md
web-speech-recognition-feature-detected	advisory	Speech recognition is obtained through a feature-detect, not by constructing the webkit-prefixed API directly	skills/web-speech-io/SKILL.md

You are auditing the Claudinite repo at /home/user/Claudinite for a "check-fix subsumption audit" (agent-preprocessing DESIGN §11, from #405).

BACKGROUND: Claudinite's per-repo `baselining` task used to run an AGENT "align" step that, for every failing conformance check, applied that check's own `fix` remedy. We are moving baselining so a deterministic PREPROCESSING worker handles the mechanical work and the agent runs only when judgment is left. The deterministic preprocessing performs exactly these four kinds of repair:
  (1) CONVERGE — rebuild `.claudinite/shared/` to the canon vendor set (vendoring/apply-vendor-set.mjs): fixes any drift where a vendored/mounted file differs from canon.
  (2) CONVERGE-WIRING (engine/scheduler/converge-wiring.mjs): fixes the scheduler workflow (.github/workflows/claudinite-scheduler.yml + its hashed cron), the settings.json SessionStart/Stop/PreToolUse hook registrations, and removal of the retired `@.claudinite/shared/CLAUDE.md` corpus-index import.
  (3) DECLARATION-NORMALIZATION: rewrites a local pack's declaration token in .claudinite-checks.json to the canonical `local/<name>` form.
  (4) MECHANICAL MIGRATIONS (migrations/apply.mjs): file aliases/renames, materialize (vendor pack templates into the repo), and literal ref rewrites declared by active migration records.

GOAL: Enumerate EVERY conformance check in the repo and classify each by whether the drift it flags is SUBSUMED by one of those four deterministic repairs, or whether it NEEDS JUDGMENT (an agent/human).

WHERE CHECKS LIVE: check rule modules are `*.mjs` files (NOT `*.test.mjs`) that export a rule object with an `id`, a `severity` ('blocking'|'advisory'), and a `run(ctx)` that returns findings via `finding(rule, {file, what, fix})`. They live under `packs/*/*.mjs`, `packs/*/skills/*/*.mjs`, `engine/checks/**`, and `.claudinite/local/packs/*/*.mjs`. Some checks are skill-owned (inside a pack's skills/<name>/). Use grep for `severity:` and `id:` and `finding(` to find them. Read each rule's `id`, `severity`, `description`, `why`, its `run()` logic, and the `fix` strings it emits.

Also check: does `engine/checks/check_the_world.mjs` run the SAME set of checks (so a failing check would always be caught by the check_the_world escalation gate)? Note any check that runs only in a different scope (e.g. work-scope only, or CI-only) and would NOT appear in a whole-repo check_the_world sweep.

For EACH check, produce a record. Classify `category` as exactly one of:
  - "converge"            (drift = a vendored/mounted file differs from canon; converge fixes it)
  - "converge-wiring"     (scheduler workflow / hooks / retired import)
  - "declaration-normalization"
  - "migrations"          (legacy shape a migration record mechanically converts)
  - "needs-judgment"      (code quality, author-written doc/reference integrity, file placement, naming, test discipline, security, or anything requiring a real decision — NOT mechanically fixable by the four repairs)
And set `subsumed` = true for the first four categories, false for needs-judgment.
For needs-judgment checks, also set `mechanicalFix` = true ONLY if the check's `fix` is actually a rote/mechanical edit that the old baselining would have auto-applied AND that is NOT yet covered by the four deterministic repairs (i.e. a candidate to make deterministic later); otherwise false.

Return your final answer as STRICT JSON (no prose around it) of shape:
{
  "checkTheWorldRunsAll": <boolean>,
  "checkTheWorldNotes": "<short note on any check NOT in the world sweep>",
  "checks": [
    {"id": "...", "pack": "...", "severity": "blocking|advisory", "category": "...", "subsumed": <bool>, "mechanicalFix": <bool>, "detects": "<one line: what drift it flags>", "rationale": "<one line: why this category>"}
  ]
}
Be exhaustive — every check module. If you are unsure of a classification, pick the closest and say so in rationale. Do not skip skill-owned checks or engine/checks/ checks.

content.text: Let me read the migration registry semantics and the chrome-release-vendoring record to judge exactly what migrations subsume.
Let me check the chrome-release-vendoring materialize list and the pack-independence migration to finalize the migrations-subsumed set.

content.content:
1	// chrome-extension-release's reusable workflows + composite actions moved out of
2	// Claudinite core (.github/) and into the pack, vendored into each consumer's own
3	// .github/ (#276). GitHub only resolves a reusable workflow / composite action
4	// from a repo's own .github/, never from a packs/… subdir, so "into the pack"
5	// means the pack holds the templates and each consumer hosts a managed copy.
6	//
7	// This record is the transition's whole machinery:
8	//   - appliesTo    — the gate: only a repo whose orchestrator is named
9	//                    "Release to Chrome Store" (the consumers). Claudinite carries
10	//                    a file of the same path named "… (reusable)", so it's skipped.
11	//   - materialize  — vendor the four reusable workflows + three composite actions
12	//                    verbatim into the consumer's .github/ (idempotent; overwrites
13	//                    on drift, so a hand-edited copy self-heals).
14	//   - rewrite      — repoint the orchestrator's three cross-repo @main calls at the
15	//                    local vendored files, preserving any per-repo build_env tweak.
16	//   - legacyPresent — fleet telemetry: still on the old shape while anything under
17	//                    .github/workflows/ references Claudinite's core release
18	//                    workflows @main.
19	//   - retire:'auto' + retireDeletesFromHome — once the retire pass sees 0 repos on
20	//                    the old shape, it deletes Claudinite's now-unused core release
21	//                    plumbing from the canon repo, then this record. The check-layer
22	//                    tolerance is driven by migrationActive('chrome-release-vendoring')
23	//                    (registry.mjs), so it vanishes in the same step this file is
24	//                    deleted — nothing inline is stranded, so 'auto' is honest.
25	//
26	// The automatic deletion is done by the migration retire pass over daily
27	// maintenance's own access to the canon repo (no special grant) — once the fleet has
28	// vendored the copy, so the canon ends with no leftovers.
29	const STUB = '.github/workflows/chrome-extension-release.yml';
30	const S = 'packs/chrome-extension-release/stubs';
31	
32	export default {
33	  id: 'chrome-release-vendoring',
34	  landed: '2026-07-13',
35	  summary: 'chrome-extension-release reusable workflows + composite actions vendored from Claudinite core into each consumer repo (#276)',
36	
37	  // Only touch a repo that ships the pipeline — its orchestrator is named
38	  // "Release to Chrome Store" (or the legacy "Release"). `read(path)` returns the
39	  // file's content or null.
40	  appliesTo: async (read) => {
41	    const text = await read(STUB);
42	    if (!text) return false;
43	    const name = /^name:\s*['"]?(.+?)['"]?\s*$/m.exec(text)?.[1];
44	    return name === 'Release to Chrome Store' || name === 'Release';
45	  },
46	
47	  // Vendor the reusable workflows + actions verbatim (the create-package reusable
48	  // is renamed from chrome-extension-release.yml to avoid colliding with the
49	  // orchestrator's own filename inside one repo).
50	  materialize: [
51	    { template: `${S}/workflows/chrome-extension-create-package.yml`, dest: '.github/workflows/chrome-extension-create-package.yml' },
52	    { template: `${S}/workflows/chrome-extension-publish-store.yml`, dest: '.github/workflows/chrome-extension-publish-store.yml' },
53	    { template: `${S}/workflows/chrome-extension-daily-release.yml`, dest: '.github/workflows/chrome-extension-daily-release.yml' },
54	    { template: `${S}/workflows/deploy-privacy-page.yml`, dest: '.github/workflows/deploy-privacy-page.yml' },
55	    { template: `${S}/actions/read-release-config/action.yml`, dest: '.github/actions/read-release-config/action.yml' },
56	    { template: `${S}/actions/read-release-config/read-config.mjs`, dest: '.github/actions/read-release-config/read-config.mjs' },
57	    { template: `${S}/actions/bump-extension-patch/action.yml`, dest: '.github/actions/bump-extension-patch/action.yml' },
58	    { template: `${S}/actions/bump-extension-patch/bump.mjs`, dest: '.github/actions/bump-extension-patch/bump.mjs' },
59	    { template: `${S}/actions/report-failure/action.yml`, dest: '.github/actions/report-failure/action.yml' },
60	  ],
61	
62	  // Repoint the orchestrator's three @main calls at the local vendored files.
63	  rewrite: [
64	    { file: STUB, replace: [
65	      { from: 'missingbulb/Claudinite/.github/workflows/chrome-extension-release.yml@main', to: './.github/workflows/chrome-extension-create-package.yml' },
66	      { from: 'missingbulb/Claudinite/.github/workflows/chrome-extension-publish-store.yml@main', to: './.github/workflows/chrome-extension-publish-store.yml' },
67	      { from: 'missingbulb/Claudinite/.github/workflows/chrome-extension-daily-release.yml@main', to: './.github/workflows/chrome-extension-daily-release.yml' },
68	    ] },
69	  ],
70	
71	  // A repo is still on the legacy shape while anything under .github/workflows/
72	  // still references Claudinite's core release workflows @main.
73	  legacyPresent: async (exists, read) => {
74	    const text = (await read(STUB)) || '';
75	    return text.includes('missingbulb/Claudinite/.github/workflows/chrome-extension-');
76	  },
77	
78	  // retire: 'manual' — the fleet has fully vendored (0 repos on the legacy shape),
79	  // but this record is NOT safe to auto-retire: deleting it + retireDeletesFromHome
80	  // strands a web of inline references the retire pass does not clean — the barriers
81	  // `except` entries in the canon's .claudinite-checks.json that excuse those core
82	  // files, the .github/workflows/README.md links to them, and this migration's own
83	  // tests (migrations.test.mjs asserts migrationActive('chrome-release-vendoring')
84	  // and the deletion-set length). Auto-retiring broke the canon's CI (53 stale-ref
85	  // findings + 2 tests). Retire it by hand in one change that also prunes those
86	  // references (and consider teaching the retire pass to sweep references, or
87	  // driving these tolerances off resolvePath, before flipping back to 'auto').
88	  retire: 'manual',
89	  // Claudinite's now-unused core release plumbing, deleted on retirement (each
90	  // file explicitly — the Contents API deletes files, not directories; an emptied
91	  // action dir drops out with its last file).
92	  //
93	  // report-failure is deliberately EXCLUDED from this deletion set: it is shared
94	  // canon infrastructure, NOT chrome-release-exclusive. Another (non-chrome) pack's
95	  // vendored coverage-workflow stub (stubs/fleet-coverage.yml) references
96	  // `…/.github/actions/report-failure@main`, and it is the general
97	  // unattended-workflow failure reporter documented across the fleet's consumer
98	  // repos. This migration's legacyPresent probe only inspects the
99	  // chrome-extension-release *stub*, so its "0 repos on the legacy shape" signal says
100	  // nothing about those non-chrome @main callers — deleting report-failure on that
101	  // signal would break that other pack's coverage workflow on its next
102	  // materialization. The four release *workflows* being deleted here
103	  // reference report-failure @main internally, but those references vanish with the
104	  // files; the canon action itself must survive for the non-chrome callers. Chrome
105	  // consumers keep working via the copy `materialize` vendors into each of them.
106	  // (Retiring report-failure itself, if ever, belongs to whatever owns that shared
107	  // action — not to this chrome-release migration.)
108	  retireDeletesFromHome: [
109	    '.github/workflows/chrome-extension-release.yml',
110	    '.github/workflows/chrome-extension-publish-store.yml',
111	    '.github/workflows/chrome-extension-daily-release.yml',
112	    '.github/workflows/deploy-privacy-page.yml',
113	    '.github/actions/read-release-config/action.yml',
114	    '.github/actions/read-release-config/read-config.mjs',
115	    '.github/actions/bump-extension-patch/action.yml',
116	    '.github/actions/bump-extension-patch/bump.mjs',
117	  ],
118	};
119

1	// Pack independence (extending.md; the canon-side `pack-independence` barrier):
2	// packs no longer import each other's code — a fixed folder-barrier is now
3	// CONTRIBUTED as manifest data (`requires` the mechanism pack +
4	// `contributes` on pack.mjs; the engine builds the rule) and the shared
5	// path/migration helpers moved into the vendored engine lib (engine/checks/helpers/).
6	// The old code-composition export (`defineBarrier`) is gone.
7	//
8	// Canon-side the conversion is complete (rule first, then the fixes — one
9	// commit each). Member-side there are no mechanical ops: nothing in a
10	// consumer's tree is renamed or rewritten. The AGENTIC note for the nightly
11	// worker: if a member's own local packs (.claudinite/local_packs/) composed a
12	// barrier by importing the shared engine, convert each to the contribution
13	// shape — move the barrier object (id, edges, description, why, doc,
14	// crossingExcuse, gateDir) onto the local pack's manifest under
15	// `contributes` and add the mechanism pack to its `requires`. A local pack
16	// still importing the removed export fails loudly at pack discovery (the
17	// runner's fail-soft config finding names the broken manifest) — that
18	// diagnostic is the member-side signal. Consumers with no such local packs
19	// need nothing.
20	//
21	// retire: 'auto' — the durable enforcement is the check, which every member
22	// runs from its own snapshot; this record only carries the one-time
23	// conversion guidance through the fleet's next cycles.
24	export default {
25	  id: 'pack-independence',
26	  landed: '2026-07-19',
27	  summary: 'packs compose by declaration + contributed config, never code imports; local packs that imported the shared barrier engine convert to `contributes` on their manifest',
28	  // The AGENTIC note above, now machine-readable (agent-preprocessing DESIGN §7):
29	  // baselining's deterministic preprocessing detects this and escalates to the
30	  // agent stage instead of advancing the stamp past it. There are no mechanical
31	  // member-side ops, so this is the record's whole member-side work.
32	  agentic: {
33	    model: 'sonnet',
34	    instructions: 'If a member local pack (.claudinite/local/packs/) composed a barrier by importing the shared engine, convert it to the contribution shape: move the barrier object (id, edges, description, why, doc, crossingExcuse, gateDir) onto the local pack manifest under `contributes`, and add the mechanism pack to its `requires`. A member with no such local pack needs nothing.',
35	  },
36	};
37

content.text: The audit enumeration is running in the background. Meanwhile, let me de-risk the **single riskiest correctness point** in E4's worker locally (no token needed): does `apply-vendor-set`, run from a **rootless** canon clone against a target stamped at an *older* ref, converge without tripping the anti-rewind guard? Let me validate that mechanism directly.

input.command:
cd /tmp/claude-0/-home-user/2d1c692e-30da-57da-8bc9-0829634e7b41/scratchpad 2>/dev/null || cd /tmp
SCRATCH=$(mktemp -d)
echo "=== 1. test public canon clone through proxy (depth 1) ==="
timeout 90 git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/missingbulb/Claudinite.git "$SCRATCH/canon" 2>&1 | tail -3
if [ -d "$SCRATCH/canon/.git" ]; then
  HEAD=$(git -C "$SCRATCH/canon" rev-parse HEAD)
  echo "canon HEAD: $HEAD"
  echo "=== 2. make it rootless (drop .git) — the worker's trick ==="
  rm -rf "$SCRATCH/canon/.git"
  [ -d "$SCRATCH/canon/.git" ] && echo "STILL HAS .git" || echo "rootless OK"
  echo "=== 3. scratch target stamped at an OLD ref (behind), packs=[basics] ==="
  mkdir -p "$SCRATCH/target"
  cat > "$SCRATCH/target/.claudinite-checks.json" <<JSON
{
  "packs": ["basics"],
  "maintenance": { "delivery": "auto-merge" },
  "claudinite": { "updated": "2026-07-01T00:00:00.000Z", "ref": "b5103ea4341a29c070a34508275809efb96b3a05" }
}
JSON
  echo "=== 4. run apply-vendor-set from the ROOTLESS canon against the target ==="
  node "$SCRATCH/canon/vendoring/apply-vendor-set.mjs" --target "$SCRATCH/target" --ref "$HEAD" 2>&1 | tail -4
  echo "=== 5. did the mount converge + stamp advance (no anti-rewind error)? ==="
  grep -A3 '"claudinite"' "$SCRATCH/target/.claudinite-checks.json"
  echo "shared file count: $(find "$SCRATCH/target/.claudinite/shared" -type f 2>/dev/null | wc -l)"
  echo "baselining worker vendored? $(ls "$SCRATCH/target/.claudinite/shared/packs/basics/tasks/baselining/worker.mjs" 2>&1)"
else
  echo "CLONE FAILED — will fall back to a rootless copy of local canon"
fi
echo "SCRATCH=$SCRATCH"

content.text:
Both partial-pilot phases are done. Here's where the four items stand — three substantially advanced, the audit still computing.

## ✅ Merges — done
- **GCEC #711** → merged to `main`.
- **Claudinite #412** → merged to `main` (now the E4/E5 mechanism is in canon; dormant until a repo's baselining converts).

## ✅ E4 pilot — I ran the do-able part locally (strong de-risk)
I validated the worker's whole converge path against a real target, no token needed — only the native-git `deliver()` (push/PR/auto-merge) still needs a real Action token:
- **Public canon clone through the proxy works** (no token) → HEAD `94553b0`.
- **The rootless-tree trick works**: `apply-vendor-set` from the de-`.git`'d clone converged a target stamped at the *older* `b5103ea` up to HEAD **without tripping the anti-rewind guard** (89 files; the new baselining `worker.mjs` landed in the converged mount). This was the single riskiest correctness point — confirmed sound.
- **converge-wiring** converged the scheduler workflow (now the v5/node24 stub) + all three hooks.
- **Mechanical migrations** applied idempotently (no-op — shape current).
- **Agentic-note + stamp-hold + escalation** verified both ways: with an old stamp the `pack-independence@2026-07-19` note is *pending* → stamp held at `2026-07-18`, agent requested ✓; with GCEC's real `2026-07-23` stamp → no pending note (so a real GCEC night escalates only if `check_the_world` is red).

**Residual pilot gate (owner):** one real Action-context run to exercise `deliver()` — the per-cycle branch push, PR creation, and auto-merge arming — which needs the `GITHUB_TOKEN` I don't have here (MCP-only session).

## ⚠️ GCEC executor routine — needs you (API can't wire label events)
The available CCR trigger API only fires on cron/run-once into a session — it can't create a **label-event** routine. Per bootstrap's documented fallback, here's the exact config for you to create in CCR:

```
Trigger:  GitHub label event — `ready-for-agent` added to an issue
Repo:     missingbulb/GoogleCalendarEventCreator
Model:    sonnet
Prompt:   Execute the Claudinite executor: .claudinite/shared/engine/scheduler/executor.md
Sources:  missingbulb/GoogleCalendarEventCreator  +  missingbulb/Claudinite (canon)
```
**Sequencing note:** keep canon in sources *for now* — GCEC's mount is still the old snapshot, so its current baselining (and `growth-dedup`) still read canon in-session. Once it baselines onto the new E4 worker and the pilot's `deliver()` step is confirmed, re-create it **project-only** (that's the E5 rollout for GCEC). Also note: creating it will immediately drain the 5 stuck `ready-for-agent` issues → 5 real agent runs. If you'd rather not kick those off yet, I can just close the stale dispatches instead.

## ⏳ Check-fix subsumption audit — in progress
The enumeration agent is still cataloguing all ~59 checks and classifying each (subsumed by converge/converge-wiring/normalization/migrations vs. needs-judgment). I'll write it up when it returns.

Want me to (a) close the 5 stale GCEC dispatches now or leave them for the executor to drain, and (b) post the pilot-validation results as a comment on #394 for the record?

lastPrompt: lgtm both. What's the next steps?

- 59 checks total: the 57 rules the engine's own `--list` enumerates, plus `promote-scope` (a CI-only standalone check, not registered in a pack's `rules` and not in the world sweep) and the runner-internal `config` load-integrity diagnostic.

- No check maps to CONVERGE (the `.claudinite/shared/` mount rebuild) — conformance rules target the consumer's authored surface, never the vendored mount content. No check maps to DECLARATION-NORMALIZATION either — the engine accepts both the bare and `local/<name>` declaration forms, so no rule flags the un-namespaced token as drift (that normalization rides baselining/the local-pack-namespace migration silently). The retired-import removal and hook registration in converge-wiring likewise have no corresponding conformance check.
- 3 needs-judgment checks have a genuinely rote fix not yet covered by the four repairs (`mechanicalFix: true`, candidates to make deterministic later): `catalog-completeness` (regenerate packs/README from the tree), `generated-merge-driver` (add the .gitattributes merge=ours line), `cer/version-sync` (set package.json version := manifest version).
- check_the_world does NOT run the same set: 8 work-scoped rules run only in check_the_work against the change/transcript and never appear in the whole-repo sweep that baselining's escalation gate uses (listed above), and `promote-scope` is CI-only.

The key repair implementations I relied on: `/home/user/Claudinite/engine/scheduler/converge-wiring.mjs`, `/home/user/Claudinite/vendoring/compute-vendor-set.mjs`, `/home/user/Claudinite/migrations/apply.mjs` + `/home/user/Claudinite/migrations/registry.mjs`, the migration records under `/home/user/Claudinite/migrations/active_migrations/`, and the orchestrating worker `/home/user/Claudinite/packs/basics/tasks/baselining/worker.mjs`.

content.text: The audit enumeration is back — and it surfaces the load-bearing finding. Let me digest it: only **2 checks** are deterministically subsumed, but the crucial insight is about the **escalation gate**. `check_the_world` (what preprocessing runs) omits **8 work-scoped checks** — the question is whether that's a silent regression. It isn't, and that's exactly what makes the null-night safe. Let me write this up as the audit deliverable.

input.content:
# Check-fix subsumption audit

Absorbed from #405, tracked in [DESIGN.md §11](DESIGN.md). **Load-bearing
question:** is it safe to make baselining's common (mechanical) night fully
**agentless** — drive its `agent_model` toward `null` — relying on the escalation
gate to summon an agent only when judgment is genuinely left?

The old baselining ran an AGENT "align" step that, for every failing conformance
check, applied that check's own `fix` remedy. E4 replaced the mechanical work with
a deterministic preprocessing worker and made the agent stage **conditional**
(agent-preprocessing DESIGN §3, §11). For that to be safe, every check the old
align step would have fixed must be **either** (a) handled deterministically by
preprocessing, **or** (b) caught by the escalation gate so it still summons the
agent. A check that is *neither* would be a **silent regression** — drift the old
model repaired that the new model neither fixes nor escalates on.

## The two things the new model relies on

1. **The four deterministic repairs** preprocessing runs
   ([`worker.mjs`](../../packs/basics/tasks/baselining/worker.mjs)):
   - **converge** — rebuild `.claudinite/shared/` to the canon vendor set
     ([`apply-vendor-set.mjs`](../../vendoring/apply-vendor-set.mjs)).
   - **converge-wiring** — the scheduler workflow + hashed cron, the settings
     hooks, and the retired-import removal
     ([`converge-wiring.mjs`](../../engine/scheduler/converge-wiring.mjs)).
   - **declaration-normalization** — the `local/<name>` pack token.
   - **mechanical migrations** — aliases / materialize / rewrite
     ([`migrations/apply.mjs`](../../migrations/apply.mjs)).
2. **The escalation gate** — preprocessing requests the agent iff a pending
   *agentic* migration note exists, **or** the converge changed something and
   `check_the_world` is **not green**.

## Finding — the classification of all 59 checks

Full per-check classification was produced by enumerating every rule module
(`packs/*`, `packs/*/skills/*`, `engine/checks/`, `.claudinite/local/packs/*`).
The shape of the result:

- **Subsumed by a deterministic repair — 2.**
  - `scheduler-workflow-shape` → **converge-wiring** rewrites that exact workflow
    to the vendored stub with the repo's hashed cron.
  - `cer/release-workflows` → the `chrome-release-vendoring` migration's
    materialize/rewrite self-heals the vendored reusable-workflows/actions drift.
    (Its orchestrator name/schedule/token assertions are one-time adoption
    authoring, not recurring nightly drift.)
- **No check maps to converge, declaration-normalization, retired-import, or
  hook-registration.** Conformance rules target the consumer's **authored**
  surface, never the vendored mount content or the wiring registrations. So those
  repairs run **unconditionally** in preprocessing with no gate needed — there is
  nothing for a check to have caught.
- **Needs-judgment — the rest (~55).** Genuine decisions: code quality, author
  doc/reference integrity, file placement, security, process discipline, pack /
  release / product-wiki authoring. These are exactly what *should* summon an
  agent, not be auto-edited by a mechanical converge.

## The load-bearing subtlety — `check_the_world` ≠ every check

The escalation gate runs **`check_the_world`** (world scope). It does **not** run
**8 work-scoped rules**, which run only in `check_the_work` against a session's
diff/transcript:

`reference-integrity`, `comment-classification`, `task-lifecycle`,
`squash-merge-history`, `feature-requirements-first`, `node/earn-each-dependency`,
`cer/permission-added-store-issue`, `google-client-id-single-origin`
(plus `promote-scope`, a CI-only gate on the growth-promote branch).

So the gate is **blind** to those 8. Are they a silent regression? **No** — and
this is the crux of why the agentless night is sound:

> A work-scoped check judges a **session's change**, not standing repo state. On an
> agentless night there is no agent session, and preprocessing's only change is the
> **mechanical converge** — vendored canon files (already clean) plus the stamp.
> That diff makes none of the moves these checks police:
> - `reference-integrity` / `markdown` — the converge writes vendored files, which
>   carry no broken author links; pre-existing dangling links in *unchanged* author
>   files were never in a work-scoped check's view (or the old align's), so leaving
>   them is not new.
> - `task-lifecycle` / `squash-merge-history` — baselining's delivery is one
>   mechanical commit that `Refs` the tracking issue on the maintenance PR, correct
>   by construction.
> - `earn-each-dependency` / `permission-added` / `google-client-id` /
>   `comment-classification` / `feature-requirements-first` — each fires on a
>   specific authored act (adding a dep, a permission, a client-id literal, replying
>   to the owner, a feature commit) that a mechanical converge never performs.

Every check that judges **standing repo state** (the drift a nightly refresh is
meant to catch) **is** world-scoped and therefore **is** in the gate. The
work-scoped exclusions judge changes the converge does not make. **No silent
regression.**

## Conclusion

**It is safe to drive baselining's common-night model toward `null`.** The
escalation gate (`check_the_world` green **and** no pending agentic note) covers
every standing-repo judgment check; the deterministic repairs cover the mount,
wiring, normalization, and mechanical migrations (none of which a check guards);
and the work-scoped checks the gate omits judge session changes a mechanical
converge never makes. Until the model is actually flipped to `null`, the gate is
already the operative safety net — a converge left non-green escalates today.

## Backlog — mechanize to reduce needless escalation (non-blocking)

Three **world-scoped** needs-judgment checks have a genuinely **rote** fix. Today a
repo failing one escalates to an agent (safe, but wasteful). Making each an
additional deterministic repair would keep more nights agentless:

- `catalog-completeness` — regenerate `packs/README.md` entries from the pack tree.
- `generated-merge-driver` — add the known `.gitattributes` `merge=ours` line for a
  GENERATED file that lacks it.
- `cer/version-sync` — set `package.json` version := the manifest version.

None blocks the null-model flip; each is an incremental way to shrink the set of
nights that need an agent at all.

lastPrompt: lgtm both. What's the next steps?

-  to a `null`-model common night.
+- **The check-fix subsumption audit** (from #405, load-bearing): **DONE** —
+  [`check-fix-subsumption-audit.md`](check-fix-subsumption-audit.md). Conclusion:
+  it **is** safe to drive the common-night model toward `null`. Every check that
+  judges standing repo state is world-scoped and so is caught by the
+  `check_the_world` escalation gate; the 8 work-scoped checks the gate omits judge a
+  *session's change* a mechanical converge never makes (no silent regression); only
+  2 checks are deterministically subsumed today, and 3 world-scoped needs-judgment
+  checks (`catalog-completeness`, `generated-merge-driver`, `cer/version-sync`) have
+  rote fixes worth mechanizing later to reduce needless escalation.
 - **Rollout sequencing** of the contract change vs. #407 vs. Phase 2 (§8) —
   ordering agreed in principle (#407 → contract → Phase 2), dates open.

input.head: claude/agent-preprocessing-remaining-kymhh9
input.title: docs: check-fix subsumption audit — clears the null-model common night
input.body:
## What & why

Resolves the load-bearing open item from #405 / [agent-preprocessing DESIGN §11](https://github.com/missingbulb/Claudinite/blob/main/docs/agent-preprocessing/DESIGN.md) (Refs #394): **is it safe to make baselining's common night agentless** (`agent_model → null`), relying on the escalation gate to summon an agent only when judgment is left?

**Conclusion: yes, safe.** Adds [`docs/agent-preprocessing/check-fix-subsumption-audit.md`](https://github.com/missingbulb/Claudinite/blob/claude/agent-preprocessing-remaining-kymhh9/docs/agent-preprocessing/check-fix-subsumption-audit.md).

## Method & findings

Enumerated and classified **all 59 conformance checks** against the four deterministic preprocessing repairs (converge / converge-wiring / declaration-normalization / mechanical migrations) and the `check_the_world` escalation gate.

- **Only 2 checks are deterministically subsumed today** — `scheduler-workflow-shape` (converge-wiring) and `cer/release-workflows` (the chrome-release-vendoring migration).
- **The mount converge, declaration-normalization, retired-import removal, and hook registration have *no* conformance checks** — they target the vendored/wiring surface, not authored content — so they run unconditionally with nothing to gate. This is why "only 2 subsumed" is not alarming: most of what the old agent-align "fixed" was judgment work that correctly escalates now.
- **The load-bearing subtlety:** the gate runs `check_the_world`, which omits **8 work-scoped checks** (`reference-integrity`, `comment-classification`, `task-lifecycle`, `squash-merge-history`, `feature-requirements-first`, `node/earn-each-dependency`, `cer/permission-added-store-issue`, `google-client-id-single-origin`). Those judge a **session's change** (a diff/transcript), not standing repo state — and a mechanical converge makes none of the moves they police. Every check that judges **standing repo state** is world-scoped and **is** in the gate. So the omission is **not** a silent regression.

## Backlog (non-blocking)

3 world-scoped needs-judgment checks have rote fixes worth mechanizing later so a repo hitting them stays agentless: `catalog-completeness`, `generated-merge-driver`, `cer/version-sync`.

## Also in this PR

- Marks the §11 open item done and links the audit.
- Records **E4's partial pilot** (2026-07-23): against a real target stamped at an older ref, the public-canon clone (through the proxy, no token) → rootless tree → `apply-vendor-set` converged to HEAD **without tripping the anti-rewind guard**; converge-wiring, mechanical migrations, and the agentic-note/stamp-hold/escalation decision all verified. Only the native-git `deliver()` (branch push, PR create, auto-merge arm) still needs one real Action-token run — the E5 gate.

## Verification

- `check_the_work` / `check_the_world` — 0 blocking (the audit doc's relative links resolve).

---

Resolves the load-bearing open item from #405/§11: is it safe to make
baselining's common night agentless (agent_model -> null), relying on the
escalation gate to summon an agent only when judgment is left?

Enumerated and classified all 59 conformance checks against the four
deterministic preprocessing repairs and the check_the_world escalation
gate. Conclusion: YES, safe.

- Only 2 checks are deterministically subsumed today (scheduler-workflow-
  shape -> converge-wiring; cer/release-workflows -> migrations). The mount
  converge, declaration-normalization, retired-import, and hook registration
  have NO conformance checks (they target the vendored/wiring surface, not
  authored content) — so they run unconditionally with nothing to gate.
- The remaining ~55 are needs-judgment — exactly what should escalate.
- Load-bearing subtlety: the gate runs check_the_world, which omits 8
  work-scoped checks. Those judge a SESSION'S CHANGE (a diff/transcript),
  not standing repo state, and a mechanical converge makes none of the moves
  they police — so their omission is not a silent regression. Every check
  that judges standing repo state IS world-scoped and IS in the gate.
- Backlog: 3 world-scoped needs-judgment checks (catalog-completeness,
  generated-merge-driver, cer/version-sync) have rote fixes worth mechanizing
  later to reduce needless escalation. Non-blocking.

Adds docs/agent-preprocessing/check-fix-subsumption-audit.md and marks the
§11 open item done; also records E4's partial pilot (converge path validated
against a real older-stamped target; only native-git deliver() remains).

Refs #394

content.text:
That closes the executor-routine item — thanks. Two practical notes on what that unlocks, then your two open questions.

**On the 5 stale dispatches:** the executor fires on a *new* `ready-for-agent` label event, and those 5 issues were labeled at 12:08 — *before* the routine existed — so their label events won't retroactively wake it. The executor's self-healing sweep (executor.md step 1) drains every open `ready-for-agent` issue, but only once *something* triggers a session. Two ways to kick it:
- I re-apply the label to one issue (remove + re-add → fires an event → the sweep drains all 5), **or**
- the next scheduler dispatch (e.g. baselining at the 02:00 slot) triggers it naturally.

Draining runs 5 real agent sessions (opus/sonnet opening PRs). I won't trigger that without your go.

**One thing worth confirming on the routine:** GCEC's mount is still the *old* snapshot, so its current baselining (and `growth-dedup`) read canon in-session. Make sure "Claudinite executor - GCEC" has **both** GCEC **and** the Claudinite canon in its sources for now — flip it to **project-only** (E5) only after GCEC baselines onto the new worker and the `deliver()` pilot passes.

**How the E4 pilot completes itself from here:** with the executor live, the next 02:00 baselining dispatch runs the *old* baselining → refreshes GCEC's mount to the new canon head → the following night, the new `worker.mjs` preprocessing runs and exercises the real `deliver()`. So the pilot lands over the next couple of nightly cycles without more setup — I just need to watch it (I can subscribe to the maintenance PR when it appears).

Your two open questions, still yours to call:
1. **Drain the 5 now** (I re-label to kick the sweep) or **close them** as stale, or **leave them** for the next scheduler tick?
2. **Post the E4 partial-pilot results as a comment on #394**?

And should I **merge #413** once its CI is green?

content.text: Let me look at the error and what GCEC's mount actually contains.

{"number":710,"title":"Executor routine is broken: .claudinite/shared/engine/scheduler/executor.md does not exist","body":"A scheduled session fired with the standard thin-pointer prompt:

> Execute the Claudinite executor: `.claudinite/shared/engine/scheduler/executor.md`

That file does not exist in this repo's vendored tree, and never has — I checked `git log --all` for it (no hits) and diffed it against the per-project-scheduling cutover commit (d0ea13b, "Phase 1 (GCEC pilot): per-project scheduling cutover", #700), which vendored `run.mjs`, `dispatch.mjs`, `validate-dispatch.mjs`, `verify-outcome.mjs`, `task-contract.mjs`, `model-map.mjs`, `hash-minute.mjs`, `slots.mjs`, `discover.mjs`, and `stubs/claudinite-scheduler.yml` — but never an `executor.md`.

This isn't cosmetic: [`scheduled-tasks.md`](.claudinite/shared/packs/basics/scheduled-tasks.md) documents a per-repo **executor routine** (fired by the `ready-for-agent` label event) as the thing that drains dispatch issues, and the [baselining task](.claudinite/local/packs/gcec/tasks/fallback-extractor-improvements/task.md) — actually [`basics/tasks/baselining/task.md`](.claudinite/shared/packs/basics/tasks/baselining/task.md) — explicitly quotes this exact thin-pointer prompt and warns: *"A repo whose executor routine was deleted keeps filing `ready-for-agent` dispatch issues that nothing ever runs."* That's exactly what's happening now: the hourly scheduler Action has correctly filed dispatch issues, but the executor has no operating instructions to run them.

**Current impact** — 6 open, unclaimed `ready-for-agent` dispatch issues, all filed at the same 2026-07-23T12:08 scheduler run, none of which anything can process:

- #708 — gcec/fallback-extractor-improvements
- #707 — tidy-repo/repo-tidy
- #706 — product-wiki/wiki-growth
- #705 — grow_with_claudinite/growth-extract
- #704 — grow_with_claudinite/growth-dedup
- #703 — grow_with_claudinite/conversation-extract

I deliberately did **not** freelance-execute these 6 tasks myself by reconstructing the executor's behavior from the surrounding code (`dispatch.mjs`, `validate-dispatch.mjs`, `verify-outcome.mjs`) — several declare `agent_model: opus` and `expected_outcome: open-pr` / `merged-pr`, this repo's maintenance delivery is `auto-merge` (no human review gate), and this session is unattended. Guessing at the missing contract and then auto-merging its own output carries real risk with nobody watching to catch a mistake.

**Fix**: `executor.md` needs to be added to the vendored `.claudinite/shared/engine/scheduler/` tree (it's canon content per `CLAUDE.md` — "vendored ... refreshed by the fleet's nightly maintenance", consumed read-only here — so the source-of-truth fix belongs in the Claudinite canon repo, then rides the next vendor refresh into this repo). Until then, the 6 dispatch issues above will keep accumulating unprocessed on every scheduler run.

input.body:
Two §394 follow-ups (Refs #394).

## 1. 🐞 Fleet-fix: `executor.md` never shipped to consumers

**Root cause of GCEC's broken executor** (missingbulb/GoogleCalendarEventCreator#710). `compute-vendor-set.mjs` strips **all** engine `*.md` as "canon-maintainer reference" — which swept up `engine/scheduler/executor.md`, the executor routine's **operating instructions**. The label-wired routine's thin-pointer prompt points at `.claudinite/shared/engine/scheduler/executor.md`, so with it stripped **every cut-over consumer's executor boots with no instructions and can drain nothing**. GCEC hit exactly this: 6 `ready-for-agent` dispatches filed, none runnable. **Baselining can't fix it** — a refresh re-runs the same computation and re-excludes the file.

- Fix: a `VENDORED_ENGINE_DOCS` whitelist (by canon-relative path) so operational engine docs ship while the blanket `.md` exclusion still drops the maintainer docs beside them. Vendor set goes 89 → 90 files (`executor.md`).
- Regression tests: the synthetic fixture proves `executor.md` vendors while a sibling engine `.md` does not, **and** a real-canon-tree assertion pins the live file so it can't silently drop again.

**Follow-on (not in this PR):** consumers already cut over (GCEC) need a mount refresh to pull `executor.md`, but GCEC's executor is deadlocked (can't run baselining to refresh). Once this merges, its mount must be bootstrapped once (converge to the fixed canon head) out-of-band.

## 2. 📋 Check-fix subsumption audit (from #405 / §11)

Adds [`docs/agent-preprocessing/check-fix-subsumption-audit.md`](https://github.com/missingbulb/Claudinite/blob/claude/agent-preprocessing-remaining-kymhh9/docs/agent-preprocessing/check-fix-subsumption-audit.md). **Conclusion: safe to drive baselining's common night toward `null`.** All 59 checks classified; every check that judges *standing repo state* is world-scoped and caught by the `check_the_world` escalation gate; the 8 work-scoped checks the gate omits judge a *session's change* a mechanical converge never makes (no silent regression). 2 checks are deterministically subsumed today; 3 (`catalog-completeness`, `generated-merge-driver`, `cer/version-sync`) have rote fixes worth mechanizing later. Also records E4's partial pilot (converge path validated; only native-git `deliver()` remains).

## Verification

- `node --test` vendoring + affected suites green; `check_the_work` / `check_the_world` 0 blocking.

---
_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)_

If you'd rather merge the `executor.md` fleet-fix **independently/faster**, say so and I'll split it into its own PR.

content.text: deleted trigger trig_01Y63V6prVnCepm2r9576BwF

Diagnosed, fixed, and pushed. Here's the full picture.

## Root cause (a fleet bug, now fixed)
`executor.md` **never shipped to any consumer**. `compute-vendor-set` strips all engine `*.md` as maintainer docs — but `executor.md` is the executor's *operating instructions*, and the routine's prompt points straight at `.claudinite/shared/engine/scheduler/executor.md`. So every cut-over repo's executor boots with no instructions. GCEC hit it first (#710). Fixed in **#413** with a `VENDORED_ENGINE_DOCS` whitelist + regression tests (real-canon assertion so it can't silently drop again).

## Your questions, answered
- **Force a "baseline now" flow?** It wouldn't help — the bug is in the *vendor-set computation*, not a stale mount. Any baseline re-excludes the file. Also there's no dedicated "baseline now" trigger anyway; baselining only fires on its `daily-2h` slot or stale-by-age.

## The deadlock, and how GCEC gets fixed
GCEC is stuck: its executor is broken → can't run baselining → can't refresh its mount to *get* the fix. So a mount refresh has to happen **out-of-band, once**:

1. **Merge #413** → canon now vendors `executor.md` (+ already carries the new E4 baselining worker).
2. **I bootstrap GCEC's mount** — converge `.claudinite/shared/` to the fixed canon head and open a GCEC PR. That single refresh lands `executor.md` **and** the new baselining `worker.mjs`. (I can push this the same way I did #711 — no token/deadlock issue, since I'm not going through the broken executor.)
3. From then on: the executor has instructions → the 6 stuck dispatches drain; and baselining is the new Action-side preprocessing worker, so it self-heals going forward.

I've kept the fix on the audit branch (#413) per the branch rule — **if you'd rather merge the `executor.md` fix on its own for speed, say the word and I'll split it into a focused PR.** Either way, once it's on `main` I'll do the GCEC mount bootstrap (step 2).

Two decisions for you:
1. **Split the executor.md fix into its own PR** for a faster independent merge, or keep it bundled in #413?
2. Once merged, **go ahead and bootstrap GCEC's mount** (step 2)? It'll also pull GCEC onto the new baselining worker — effectively starting the E4 pilot for real.

content.text:
Verified — all 5 dispatches (**#703, #704, #706, #707, #708**) are **still `ready-for-agent`**, none claimed (`agent-running`), none converged. So:

**CCR execution test result: the routine fired, but execution FAILED — and that's the diagnostic that found the bug.**
- ✅ The CCR **wiring works**: the `labeled` event on #708 did wake a session (that's why you saw it run at all).
- ❌ The session **couldn't execute**: `executor.md` isn't in GCEC's mount, so it had no instructions — it filed **#710** and you stopped it. Nothing got claimed or swept, so **no `needs-human` conversions** from a sweep (the only failure artifact is #710 itself).

So the test did exactly its job: it proved the label→session trigger is sound and surfaced the real blocker — the fleet `executor.md` vendoring bug, now fixed in **#413**.

Nothing further to do on this check-in (it was my earlier scheduled poll; I've cancelled the trigger). The path forward is unchanged and waiting on your two calls from my last message:
1. **Split the `executor.md` fix into its own PR** for a faster merge, or keep it in #413?
2. After it merges, **bootstrap GCEC's mount** (lands `executor.md` + the new baselining worker → executor works, and starts the real E4 pilot)?

message.content:
In `vendoring/compute-vendor-set.mjs` at line 42:
```javascript
const VENDORED_ENGINE_DOCS = new Set(['engine/scheduler/executor.md']);
```
What's going on here? Why is this file treated specially? It's a file in engine/shcduler why is it not just like any other?


Make sure status is update in the tracking issue for later follow up in different session.

content.text:
Good questions — let me answer the design question, then update the tracking issue and the pickup doc for a future session.

## Why `executor.md` is special

The vendor set's engine rule is "copy every engine file **except** `*.md`." The assumption baked into that rule is **`.md` = maintainer documentation** — the `DESIGN.md`/`README.md` files sitting next to the code, which are reference for people working *on* the canon, not needed by a consumer that just *runs* the engine. Consumers read those upstream; shipping them would bloat every mount with docs nobody reads at runtime.

`executor.md` breaks that assumption. It's markdown not because it's documentation, but because it's an **LLM agent's prompt** — the executor is a CCR agent whose entire instruction set *is* that markdown file, and the routine's launcher prompt points at it **in the consumer's mount** (`.claudinite/shared/engine/scheduler/executor.md`). So it's the one engine `.md` that is **operational runtime data**, not reference. The `.mjs` files are code the engine executes; `executor.md` is prose an agent executes — same "must ship" status, different extension.

So the whitelist is honest about a leaky abstraction: "engine `.md` is maintainer-reference **except** the operational ones a session reads at runtime." Today `executor.md` is the only such file (run/dispatch/etc. are `.mjs`; task prompts live in `packs/` and ship as pack payload). If more operational engine `.md` appear, they join the set — or we'd revisit with an explicit marker instead of a path list. I kept it a minimal path whitelist rather than restructure, since it's a one-file exception.

Now the status updates. First the DESIGN §11 pickup doc:

- **E5 — drop canon from the executor CCR session (NEXT, gated on E4's pilot).** `executor.md`

**Live-pilot checklist for E4 (owner, before E5):** run baselining's `worker.mjs` against a
real GCEC scheduler run — confirm the public canon clone, the converge writes, the per-cycle

input.new_string:
- **FLEET-FIX — `executor.md` was never vendored (LANDED in this branch/#413).** Root cause of
  GCEC's broken executor (`GoogleCalendarEventCreator#710`): `compute-vendor-set` stripped ALL
  engine `*.md`, including `engine/scheduler/executor.md` — the executor routine's operating
  instructions. So **every cut-over consumer's executor booted with no instructions and could
  drain nothing.** Fixed with a `VENDORED_ENGINE_DOCS` whitelist + regression tests. Baselining
  can't self-heal this (a refresh re-excludes the file), so the fix must **merge**, then each
  cut-over consumer's mount needs a one-time out-of-band refresh to pull it (below).
- **GCEC mount bootstrap (NEXT, deadlock-breaker).** GCEC is stuck: broken executor → can't run
  baselining → can't refresh its mount to *get* the fix. After #413 merges, converge GCEC's
  `.claudinite/shared/` to the fixed canon head out-of-band (push a GCEC maintenance PR the same
  way, NOT through the executor) — that single refresh lands `executor.md` **and** the new E4
  baselining `worker.mjs`. Then the executor works (the 6 stuck dispatches drain) and baselining
  becomes the Action-side worker. This refresh **is** the start of the real E4 pilot.
- **E5 — drop canon from the executor CCR session (gated on E4's pilot + the GCEC bootstrap).**
  `executor.md` sources become the project alone; update bootstrap Part 6 and the executor-routine
  creation to provision a project-only environment. Keep canon in GCEC's executor sources until it
  baselines onto the new worker, THEN re-create project-only.

**Live-pilot checklist for E4 (owner, before E5):** run baselining's `worker.mjs` against a
real GCEC scheduler run — confirm the public canon clone, the converge writes, the per-cycle
maintenance branch/PR, auto-merge arming, the stamp-hold on the pending `pack-independence`
agentic note, and that a clean night is truly agentless (no `ready-for-agent` filed).
**Partial pilot already done** (converge path validated locally against a real older-stamped
target; only the native-git `deliver()` needs a real Action-token run) — see the E4 bullet above.

input.body:
Root cause confirmed and fixed upstream. `executor.md` is present in the Claudinite canon but was **excluded from every consumer's vendored mount**: `compute-vendor-set.mjs` strips all engine `*.md` as maintainer reference, which swept up `engine/scheduler/executor.md` — the executor routine's operating instructions. So this was never GCEC-specific; every cut-over consumer's executor was equally broken. Baselining can't self-heal it (a refresh re-runs the same computation and re-excludes the file).

**Fix:** missingbulb/Claudinite#413 adds a `VENDORED_ENGINE_DOCS` whitelist so `executor.md` ships while the blanket `.md` exclusion still drops the maintainer docs, plus a real-canon regression test so it can't silently drop again.

**Remaining to close this out (out-of-band, because the broken executor deadlocks self-refresh):** after #413 merges, converge GCEC's `.claudinite/shared/` to the fixed canon head via a normal maintenance PR (not through the executor). That lands `executor.md` + the new baselining worker; the executor then has instructions and the 6 stuck `ready-for-agent` dispatches (#703–#708) drain. Leaving this issue open until that refresh lands.

- **Tracking issue updated** — posted a full session-status comment on **#394** (the master tracker): what's landed/merged, what's in #413, the front-of-line next steps (merge #413 → GCEC mount bootstrap → E5), and the two open owner decisions.

- **Pickup doc updated** — DESIGN §11 "Remaining work — start here" now records the `executor.md` fleet-fix and the GCEC mount-bootstrap deadlock-breaker, so a fresh session picks up exactly where this one left off (pushed to #413).

Everything's committed and pushed; #413 now carries the audit + the `executor.md` fleet-fix + the §11 update.

Still yours to call whenever:
1. **Split the `executor.md` fix into its own PR** for a faster independent merge, or keep it bundled in #413?
2. Once merged, **go ahead with the GCEC mount bootstrap** (lands `executor.md` + the new worker, drains the stuck dispatches, and starts the real E4 pilot)?
